content.text:
{
  "dimension": "effectiveness-depth",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "The client clearly shifts across the 15 turns. She starts gripped by a serious-sounding question about whether her voice is valuable ('Maybe I feel like I don't have valuable input'), then by turn 7 the scorecard has already begun to wobble ('I don't know why I think it's a problem'). By turn 9 she smiles and names the actual fear ('that's the thing I'm afraid of... yet I know I don't have any control over that') — a felt recognition, not an intellectual reframe. The embodied experiment in turn 12-13 produces the somatic/affective shift the breakthrough condition points to: she says the control statement out loud and laughs — 'it feels ridiculous' / 'totally absurd. I totally get it.' The deeper layer (control-seeking under the value-question) was reached and felt, not merely named. Expected territory (invert-the-question, mortality frame, embodied experiment, self-as-fellow-traveler) was traversed, and the coach avoided the reassurance trap entirely.",
  "evidence_turns": [1, 3, 7, 9, 13, 15],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 1, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Opening: client presents the surface question about whether her voice has value."},
    {"turn": 3, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Client states the belief: 'I don't have valuable input' — the scorecard is fully in place."},
    {"turn": 5, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Client notices the question isn't answerable — first crack in the frame."},
    {"turn": 7, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "'I don't know why I think it's a problem' — the premise is loosening from her side."},
    {"turn": 9, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Smile + naming the actual fear of being boring + acknowledging she has no control. A felt recognition surfaces."},
    {"turn": 13, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Embodied experiment lands: saying it aloud produces laughter and 'it feels ridiculous' — somatic/affective shift."},
    {"turn": 15, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Closing language has changed entirely from turn 1: 'totally absurd. I totally get it.' The grip is gone."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["invert-the-question", "dissolve-the-frame-with-mortality", "embodied-experiment", "self-as-fellow-traveler-not-authority", "whats-the-case-against-it", "what-makes-you-care-about-the-outcome", "self-importance-as-defense"],
  "expected_territory_misses": [],
  "notes": "Short conversation but the breakthrough condition is unambiguously met in client turns 9 and 13."
}
